title: Control Board Internal Failure
shortDescription: The electronic oven control board has failed an internal self-test.
fullDescription: Error F1 on Dacor ovens indicates the electronic oven control (EOC) has detected an internal hardware failure. Dacor was acquired by Samsung in 2016, but pre-acquisition models use control systems similar to other domestic manufacturers. The oven will not operate until the board is replaced.

causes.cause: Control board processor or EEPROM failure

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes. If F1 was a temporary glitch, it may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the control board and reseat all connectors. Look for heat damage.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for visible damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned components or swollen capacitors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Document wire positions. Swap board and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: F1 typically requires board replacement. Dacor recommends their authorized service network.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F1 control board error fix

faq.q: Is Dacor now part of Samsung?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung acquired Dacor in 2016. Newer models may use Samsung-style error codes while older models use F-codes.

faq.q: Where can I find Dacor parts?
faq.a: Dacor parts are available from authorized dealers and specialty appliance parts retailers.
Yes. Dacor control boards are $150-$400, reflecting the premium brand pricing.

title: Oven Over-Temperature
shortDescription: The oven has exceeded the maximum safe temperature limit.
fullDescription: Error F2 indicates the oven cavity has exceeded its safety threshold. The control shuts down heating elements. This is a critical safety code.

causes.cause: Heating relay stuck closed on the board

fixSteps.title: Cool the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and open door carefully. Wait 30-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Should read 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor if faulty
fixSteps.instruction: One mounting screw inside oven. Pull out and install replacement.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: If sensor is good, the board relay is stuck. Board replacement required.

whenToCallPro: If sensor is good, stuck relay is a fire hazard. Disconnect power immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F2 over temperature error fix

faq.a: Yes. An overheating oven is a fire hazard. Disconnect power.
Yes, self-clean can trigger F2 if the sensor has drifted.
Dacor recommends using self-clean sparingly and preferring manual cleaning with approved products.

title: Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor has an open circuit.
fullDescription: Error F3 indicates the temperature sensor has failed with an open circuit. The oven cannot read temperature and will not heat.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access rear panel. Ensure sensor connector is plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Should read 1080-1100 ohms at room temp. OL means open.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace wires for breaks near oven cavity.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: One screw inside oven. Pull out old, install new.

whenToCallPro: If new sensor does not clear F3.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F3 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Is the sensor easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes. One of the simplest oven repairs.

faq.q: Can I use the oven with F3?
faq.a: No. Without a sensor, temperature cannot be regulated.

faq.q: What type of sensor does Dacor use?
faq.a: Most Dacor ovens use an RTD type sensor reading approximately 1080-1100 ohms at room temperature.

title: Temperature Sensor Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor has a short circuit.
fullDescription: Error F4 indicates the sensor circuit has near-zero resistance. The control interprets this as extreme heat and shuts down.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Should read 1080-1100 ohms. Near zero means shorted.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Dry connector with compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Look for pinched bare wires.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Standard sensor replacement.

whenToCallPro: If new sensor does not fix F4.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F4 sensor shorted error fix

faq.a: Yes. Condensation in the connector can short the circuit.

faq.q: Is the fix the same as F3?
faq.a: Yes. Same sensor, same procedure.

faq.q: Are Dacor and Samsung sensors interchangeable?
faq.a: Newer Samsung-era Dacor models may use different parts. Verify by model number.

title: Door Latch Error
shortDescription: The door latch mechanism has failed to lock or unlock properly.
fullDescription: Error F5 indicates the door lock motor or latch switch is not functioning. Self-clean mode cannot operate without confirmed door lock.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean latch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove debris from latch mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Test latch switches
fixSteps.instruction: Test continuity in both positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and install new latch.

whenToCallPro: If door is stuck locked after power reset.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F5 door latch error fix

faq.a: Usually yes. F5 only affects self-clean.

faq.q: How do I unlock a stuck door?
faq.a: Power reset should free it. If not, look for manual release.
Yes. Dacor has a network of factory-trained service providers.

title: Stuck Touchpad Key
shortDescription: A touchpad key is stuck in a pressed state.
fullDescription: Error F7 indicates a specific key is registering as continuously pressed. The control receives constant input, preventing normal operation.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Clean with damp cloth. Dry thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: If F7 clears without touchpad, it is the problem.

fixSteps.title: Replace touchpad
fixSteps.instruction: Install new touchpad membrane.

whenToCallPro: If replacing touchpad does not fix F7.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F7 stuck key touchpad error fix

faq.a: Only by turning off the breaker.
Yes. Order the exact part for your model.
Yes, humidity or steam can cause false touches.

title: Configuration Error
shortDescription: The control board EEPROM data is corrupted.
fullDescription: Error F8 indicates EEPROM corruption on the control board. The board cannot operate without valid calibration data.

fixSteps.title: Extended power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Verify board
fixSteps.instruction: Check part number matches your model.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Reseat all connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: F8 requires board replacement.

whenToCallPro: F8 almost always requires board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F8 EEPROM configuration error fix

faq.a: Not in the field. Board replacement required.
Power surges or interruptions during write operations.
F8 is uncommon but well-documented across all oven brands.

title: Door Lock Timeout
shortDescription: The door lock motor did not complete its cycle within the time limit.
fullDescription: Error F9 indicates the door lock motor ran but the latch switch never confirmed the expected state within the allowed time. Self-clean cannot operate.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clean latch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove debris.

fixSteps.title: Test lock switch
fixSteps.instruction: Test continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace lock assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Install new lock.

whenToCallPro: If door is stuck after power reset.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F9 door lock timeout error fix

faq.q: Is F9 the same as F5?
faq.a: Similar. F5 is a switch error. F9 is a motor timeout.
Usually yes, F9 only affects self-clean.
No more than once per month.

title: Runaway Temperature
shortDescription: The oven temperature is rising uncontrollably beyond the set point.
fullDescription: Error F10 is a critical safety code. The oven is heating beyond the set temperature and the control cannot stop it. A stuck relay allows continuous power to an element.

causes.cause: Relay welded closed on the board

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker. This is a fire hazard.

fixSteps.title: Cool the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Wait at least 1 hour.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If sensor is fine, relay is stuck.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: Board replacement required. Also check elements.

whenToCallPro: F10 is a fire hazard. Call immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F10 runaway temperature error fix

faq.a: Yes. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: How is F10 different from F2?
faq.a: F2 detects over-temp. F10 means the control cannot stop heating — more severe.

faq.q: Should I check elements before replacing the board?
faq.a: Yes. A shorted element can damage a new board.

title: Relay Board Failure
shortDescription: A heating element relay on the control board has failed.
fullDescription: Error F6 indicates a bake or broil relay has failed. It may be stuck open or stuck closed, preventing proper heating control. A stuck closed relay is a fire hazard.
symptoms: Oven heats continuously and will not stop
Clicking from the control board area

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test elements first
fixSteps.instruction: Check bake (20-50 ohms) and broil (15-40 ohms) resistance before replacing the board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burned relay area.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, swap board, reconnect wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the oven heats continuously, disconnect power and call a professional immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Dacor oven F6 relay board failure fix

faq.a: Yes. A stuck closed relay means continuous uncontrolled heating. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: Should I check elements before the board?
faq.a: Yes. A shorted element can damage a new board.

faq.q: Can the relay be individually replaced?
faq.a: Some repair services offer board-level relay replacement at lower cost.
